_publications._publicationTitle: Programming of Schwann Cells by Lats1/2-TAZ/YAP Signaling Drives Malignant Peripheral Nerve Sheath Tumorigenesis.

_contextSnippet: Consistently, Lats1 or Lats2 KO nerves exhibited augmented levels of TAZ, YAP and their DNA-binding co-factor partners such as TEAD1...and their downstream targets Ctgf, Cyr61, and Ccnd1
details: Lats1/2 KO nerves exhibited augmented levels of TAZ, YAP and TEAD1, and their downstream targets Ctgf, Cyr61, and Ccnd1
